data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Slips on Tech Selloff, Rising U.S. Trade Deficit
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Wednesday, although the index is holding up much better than the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) given its lower exposure to tech stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Microsoft (MSFT), which is down by over 10% after reporting its earnings, accounts for 5.34% of the Dow. Salesforce (CRM) is also bringing down the index on fears that AI will disrupt the software industry.

Meanwhile, the Commerce Department announced the U.S. trade deficit was $56.8 billion in November, well above the consensus estimate of $44 billion and nearly doubling from $29.2 billion in the prior month. Imports increased by 5% to $348.9 billion, while exports fell by 3.6% to $292.1 billion. That doesn't bode well for gross domestic product (GDP), as rising imports reduce net exports, which directly slows GDP growth.

In another negative reading, initial jobless claims for the week ended January 24 fell by 1,000 to 209,000, higher than the consensus estimate of 205,000. In addition, the Department of Labor (DOL) revised claims for the previous week to 210,000 from 200,000. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs and document the number of people filing for unemployment benefits for the first time.

Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by one week and track the number of people continuing to receive unemployment benefits, fell by 38,000 to 1.827 million, below the estimate of 1.850 million. The DOL revised the prior week's figure to 1.865 million from 1.849 million.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.24% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BTC, ETH, DOGE: SEC Chair Says 'Time Is Right' for Pension Funds to Add Crypto
data.description:
Paul Atkins, Chair of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), says "the time is right" for retirement plans such as 401(k)s to add cryptocurrencies.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Atkins made the comments in an appearance on CNBC as the U.S. Senate considers draft legislation pertaining to cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Dogecoin (DOGE), and the ways in which digital assets are regulated.

"A lot of people are already exposed to crypto and other sorts of assets like that through their pension funds," Atkins said in the interview. "I think the time is right to go forward with that," referring to adding cryptocurrencies more broadly to people's pension plans.

Cautions on Crypto

Until now, the U.S. government and regulators had cautioned against adding Bitcoin and other crypto to pension plans, deeming the assets too risky. The U.S. Department of Labor previously said that asset managers should "exercise extreme care before they consider adding a cryptocurrency option to a 401(k) plan's investment menu for plan participants."

However, last summer, U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order that now allows crypto investments in 401(k) retirement plans, opening up access to an estimated $10 trillion asset class. The White House has said that alternative assets such as crypto offer competitive returns and diversification benefits to retirement plans.

Is Bitcoin a Buy?

Most analysts don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of BTC. As one can see in the chart below, the price of Bitcoin has declined 19.54% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Encourage More Visits": Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Slips With Rebuilt Loyalty Program
data.description:
The loyalty program is one of marketing's greatest moves. Offering people free stuff for visiting is a plan that all but ensures return business. Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) has used the loyalty program for years, though it recently moved to rebuild the program to work in concert with the larger Back to Starbucks initiative. The move did not sit well with investors, though, who sent shares sliding nearly 1.5% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, the Starbucks Rewards program will now bring in tiers, which in turn should encourage customers to come back more often. Long-time Starbucks customers are shaking their heads in amusement right now, because Starbucks had a tiered customer loyalty system back in 2019. Starbucks shut down the tiered system, claiming that the move would be more welcoming for new members and make them feel "more engaged."

But now, Starbucks is back to tiers, believing that the tier-less system does not offer due reward to the frequent Starbucks customer. Tressie Lieberman, chief brand officer, noted, "Our very best customers are coming 200 times a year, and we were treating them the same as someone who comes once a year." The tier system starts at green tier, with free goodies on the holder's birthday. The gold tier offers increased rewards points. The reserve tier, meanwhile, opens up access to events as well as a further increase in points.

Around the World

Meanwhile, Starbucks also wants to get more customers in locations around the world, and will do so by offering more local fare. Starbucks International CEO Brady Brewer noted that a saffron tiramisu he tried at a local store proved a welcome delight, and he wondered why this sort of thing was not available in more places.

Thus, Brewer figured, it was a good time to focus more on local offerings. While coffee was more of a global item, the individual touches that local stores could offer—like that saffron tiramisu—would be a great way to bolster business in the local shops. Brewer called this "…a celebration of the local culture within the context of a global company."

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 14 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 12.7% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $99.29 per share implies 6.31% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is The Stock Market Down Today, 1/29/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are deep in the red on Thursday, driven by concerns over AI spending and weakness in software stocks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), and Microsoft (MSFT) reported their earnings after the close yesterday, with all three companies announcing significant boosts in capital expenditures (CAPEX). For 2026, Meta expects between $115 and $135 billion in CAPEX compared to $72.2 billion in 2025. Tesla forecasts 2026 CAPEX of $20 billion, while Microsoft reported spending $37.5 billion during the fourth quarter, up by 66% year-over-year.

Stocks React to Software Fears and a Rising U.S. Trade Deficit

Software stocks, including MSFT, are also bringing down the benchmark index on fears that AI will disrupt the industry and lower profit margins. Those concerns intensified after ServiceNow (NOW) reported its earnings. Despite a triple-beat on revenue, adjusted earnings per share (EPS), and guidance, NOW stock is down by over 10% today.

Elsewhere, the U.S. trade deficit for November came in at $56.8 billion, above the consensus estimate of $44 billion and nearly doubling from $29.2 billion in October. That doesn't bode well for gross domestic product (GDP) because rising imports reduce net exports, which directly weighs on economic growth.

data.title: MARA, RIOT, CLSK: Bitcoin Miner Stocks Fall as Crypto Prices Crash
data.description:
The stocks of leading Bitcoin (BTC) mining companies are down sharply on Jan. 29 as the price of BTC plunges 5% to $85,000.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ethereum">Bitcoin is now down 33% from an all-time high of $126,000 reached in early October of last year. The continued slump is weighing on the shares of companies that mine the largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization.

On Jan. 29, the stocks of MARA Holdings (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT), and CleanSpark (CLSK) are down between 6% and 9% amid a broad selloff in cryptocurrencies. The price of Ethereum (ETH), the second biggest crypto, is down 6% on the day and trading at $2,800.

Bitcoin's Continued Weakness

Bitcoin has steadily declined over the last four months. Now, it appears that the price of BTC is following technology stocks lower as they sink after Microsoft (MSFT) posted disappointing financial results. Crypto is also under pressure after the U.S. Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Jan. 28.

The accelerated selling in the crypto market is having an outsized impact on mining companies such as MARA Holdings and Riot Platforms as their profitability is closely aligned with Bitcoin's price. A new report says that more than half of Bitcoin investors are now underwater in their holdings.

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will AXP, XOM, VZ Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
On Friday, American Express (AXP), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Verizon (VZ) will wrap up an eventful week of earnings. AXP and XOM will report before the market opens, while Verizon's earnings will come after the closing bell. Traders are already weighing in on their expectations through Polymarket.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • American Express is expected to report an EPS of $3.53
  • Exxon Mobil is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $1.69
  • Verizon is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $1.05

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will American Express Beat Earnings?

American Express has beaten earnings during 15 of the last 18 quarters. Polymarket traders are confident that the financial services giant will top its estimated EPS of $3.53, giving it an 88% chance.

Will Exxon Mobil Beat Earnings?

Exxon Mobil has beaten earnings 77.77% of the time during the past 18 quarters. That nearly lines up with the 79% odds of the oil and gas leader surpassing its estimated adjusted EPS of $1.69 this quarter on Polymarket.

Will Verizon Beat Earnings?

Verizon has beaten earnings during 16 of the past 18 quarters for an 88.88% success rate. Polymarket gives the company an 80% chance of surpassing its estimated adjusted EPS of $1.05.

It's worth pointing out that Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Inks $1.12 Billion Deal with 'Gene-Editing' Firm Seamless
data.description:
U.S. pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly (LLY) has signed an agreement worth up to $1.12 billion with Seamless Therapeutics, a privately held German startup company that specializes in gene editing.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Eli Lilly said it plans to work with Seamless to develop and commercialize ‌treatments for hearing loss using the biotech firm's gene-editing platform. The deal gives Eli Lilly access to Seamless' technology that engineers enzymes to correct gene mutations that lead to hearing loss in people.

These enzymes, called "programmable recombinases," are designed to make changes to a person's DNA that help prevent hearing loss. Eli Lilly said it will oversee ‌preclinical testing through to commercialization of the gene-editing platform and hearing loss treatment.

Eli Lilly's Diverse Pipeline

Eli Lilly's $1.12 billion investment in Seamless includes an upfront payment, funding for research and development, as well as future payments upon completing certain development and commercial milestones, said the two companies in a news release.

While Eli Lilly gets a lot of attention for its Zepbound weight-loss medication, the company has a diverse pipeline of new medicines in development, including related to ⁠genetic treatments for several diseases. In 2025, Eli Lilly spent $1.3 billion to buy Verve Therapeutics and acquire its gene-editing ‍therapies that help reduce ⁠high cholesterol in people with heart disease.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,191.37 implies 12.08% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
